title: paspalum sanguinale var. timorense
intro: Paspalum sanguinale var. timorense, also known as the Blood Grass, is a type of grass belonging to the Paspalum genus. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The Blood Grass is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating skin conditions such as eczema and acne. In herbal preparations, the leaves and roots of the Blood Grass are often made into teas, infusions, or poultices to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
